# Compression Truth Bias — Project Memory

## Publication
- [project_neurips_submission.md](project_neurips_submission.md) — NeurIPS 2026, Sydney, Dec 6-12. Abstract May 4, paper May 6, notification Sep 24
- [reference_fenogenova_review.md](reference_fenogenova_review.md) — Alena Fenogenova's review of v2, basis for v3 revisions

## Project Status
- Experiment 1 (math, tiny models): COMPLETE. 35 models trained.
- Experiment 2 (observations): COMPLETE. 20 models. Weak effect confirmed.
- Experiment 3 (conditions C/D/E): COMPLETE. 12 models trained.
- Experiment 4 (scaling): COMPLETE. Small (11M) 4 seeds, medium (26M) 4 seeds, large (86M) 4 random + 1 coherent.
- Experiment F (multi-rule): COMPLETE. 16 models (N=2,3,5,10 × 4 seeds).
- Experiment G (synthetic world, natural language): COMPLETE. 8 models (random + coherent × 4 seeds).
- Experiment: World multi-alt (N=2,4,8,16): COMPLETE. 16 models. Gradual rise 40%→60%, no phase transition.
- Experiment: World contradictory: COMPLETE. 4 models. acc≈47.5%, no truth bias.
- Paper draft: `paper_draft_ru.md` (Russian), `paper_draft_en.md` (English)
- Experiment I (chained tasks): COMPLETE. 4 tiny + 4 small + 2 large = 10 models.
  - Tiny: 70.9%±1.2%, Small: 64.2%±1.5%, Large: 60.6%±1.2%
  - INVERSE SCALING: accuracy DROPS with model size (opposite of random errors)
  - KEY RESULT: compressor power is double-edged sword
- **STATUS: PUBLISHED on arXiv — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.11749. 160+ models. All experiments complete.**
- Experiment K (Wikipedia entity substitution): COMPLETE. 32 models (4 sizes × 2 modes × 4 seeds). MLX on M4 Pro.
  - Random: tiny 69.6%±0.1%, small 70.7%±0.4%, medium 71.5%±0.8%, large 71.4%±0.7%
  - Coherent: tiny 48.7%±0.5%, small 46.6%±0.4%, medium 46.4%±0.8%, large 45.9%±1.5%
  - KEY: truth bias confirmed on REAL Wikipedia text with NER entity substitution
  - Weaker than math (71% vs 89%) but highly significant across all 16 random seeds
  - Coherent errors: model slightly PREFERS them (<50%), consistent with math results
  - Per-type: GPE/LOC/NORP best (77-82%), ORG/PERSON moderate (65-70%), CARDINAL weakest (61%)
- Experiment J (denoising): COMPLETE. 48 models (14 tiny + 14 small + 14 medium + 10 large). PyTorch on Modal.com T4 GPU.
  - J1 (1:1 random): tiny 65.3% → small 74.6% → medium 81.1% → large 85.2%
  - J2 (1:1 coherent): tiny 43.5% → small 44.5% → medium 45.8% → large 51.0% (at chance)
  - J3 (1:2 random): tiny 59.0% → small 68.6% → medium 73.6% → large 75.2%
  - J4 (1:4 random): tiny 56.6% → small 64.9% → medium 66.6% → large 65.8% (plateau)
  - J5 (no correct): tiny 53.8% → small 59.9% → medium 58.1% → large 56.1%
  - KEY: signal extraction scales with model size; coherent errors remain at chance; J4 plateaus
  - Standard vs denoising J1: 83→65% (tiny), 88→75% (small), 81% (medium), 89→85% (large) — gap closes with scale

## Key Results
- Random errors 50/50: ΔLoss = +0.0115 (4/4 seeds)
- Coherent errors 50/50: ΔLoss = −0.0004 (0/4 seeds) — NO truth bias
- Contradictory errors 50/50: ΔLoss = +0.0005 (4/4 seeds) — weak bias
- Breakpoint at ~15% correct data (10/90 inverts)
- **Phase 2 (observations):** ΔLoss = +0.0002 to +0.0008 (0-50% obs). WARNING confirmed: discrepancies are regular/compressible. 100% obs breaks training (loss ~0.32).
- **Paired eval (random 50/50):** ΔLoss = +0.048, accuracy = 83%, p<1e-6. Effect 4× stronger than corpus-level.
- **Paired eval (random proportions):** 40/60→79%, 30/70→75%, 20/80→69%, all p<1e-6
- **Paired eval (coherent 50/50):** ΔLoss = −0.002, accuracy = 47%, p≈1.0. No truth bias.
- **Paired eval (contradictory 50/50):** ΔLoss ≈ 0, accuracy = 49%. At noise level.
- **Paired eval (C/D/E):** accuracy ≈ 49% — NO truth bias. Corpus-level ΔLoss was artifact of text statistics.
- KEY INSIGHT: Corpus-level eval for C/D/E was confounded. Only random errors produce real truth bias.
- Per-type accuracy (random paired): algebra 99.9% > arithmetic 94% > derivative 72% > equation 65%
- **Paired eval 10/90:** ΔLoss = +0.017, accuracy = 67%, p<1e-88. Truth bias PERSISTS even at 10/90 in paired eval (corpus-level inverts).
- **Scaling (tiny→small→medium):** Paired accuracy 83.6%→88.4%→88.5%, ΔLoss +0.048→+0.063→+0.067. Truth bias INCREASES with model size but accuracy plateaus at medium.
- **Scaling coherent (small):** accuracy=49.6%, no truth bias — same as tiny. Coherent errors remain indistinguishable at any size.
- **Scaling large (86M):** 4 seeds: 88.3%, 89.4%, 88.7%, 90.0%. Mean=89.1%±0.6%, ΔLoss=+0.070. Coherent large: acc=51.8%, no bias.
- **Scaling trend:** tiny 83.1% → small 88.4% → medium 88.4% → large 89.1%. Truth bias increases with size.
- **Multi-rule (Exp F):** N=2→87.4%, N=3→89.4%, N=5→89.9%, N=10→91.5%, all p<1e-6.
  - Accuracy HIGHER than random (83.6%) for all N≥2 — multi-rule errors less compressible than purely random.
  - Monotonically increases with N. Phase transition at N=1→2 (49%→87%).
- **Synthetic world (Exp G, natural language):**
  - Random errors 50/50: acc=57.7%±1.7%, ΔLoss=+0.034 (all 4 seeds p<0.001). Effect WEAKER than math (83→58%) but significant.
  - Coherent errors 50/50: acc=46.6%±1.7% (below chance). No truth bias — same pattern as math.
  - Per-type: minerals best (65-71%), plants (56-68%), animals near chance, potions near chance.
  - Key: truth bias TRANSFERS to natural language but is weaker. Coherent errors still undetectable.
- **Coherent proportions paired eval:**
  - 50/50→47.2%, 40/60→27.8%, 30/70→14.7%, 20/80→9.6%
  - Model actively prefers coherent errors when they're majority (91% at 20/80!)
  - vs random: 50/50→83%, 40/60→79%, 30/70→75%, 20/80→69%
  - Key: truth has NO privilege when errors are equally compressible — pure frequency wins
- **Cross-domain falsification (Exp H):** COMPLETE. 16 models.
  - Derivative accuracy: 0%→35.2%, 10%→39.4%, 25%→56.0%, 50%→45.4%
  - Other types unchanged (~48-51%). Cross-domain tasks ADDRESS only derivative coherence.
  - Effect non-monotonic: peak at 25%, drops at 50% (corpus dilution)
  - Key: first evidence that cross-domain data can break coherent errors SELECTIVELY
  - Files: `data/generate_math_crossdomain.py`, `run_crossdomain.sh`

## Experiment Design (5 conditions for false theory)
- A: No observations (baseline, done) → ΔLoss = +0.0005 (4/4 seeds, weak positive)
- B: Bare discrepancies (done, Exp 2) → ΔLoss weak (+0.0005-0.0008) ✓
  - Discrepancies ARE regular/compressible as warned
- C: Ad hoc escape hatches → ΔLoss = +0.0025 (4/4) ✓
- D: Systematic correction → ΔLoss = +0.0026 (4/4) — SURPRISE: D ≈ C, not D ≈ 0
- E: Vague predictions → ΔLoss = +0.0015 (4/4) ✓
- Actual order: D ≈ C > E > B > A (expected C > B > E > D ≈ A)
- Key insight: correction step itself is the information cost, not just ad-hoc-ness
- Corpora for C/D/E already generated at `data/corpus/train_cond{C,D,E}_50_50.txt`
- Test sets: `test_correct_dual.txt`, `test_incorrect_dual.txt`

## Exp F Results (multi-rule / conspiratorial errors) — COMPLETE
- N rules per task type, randomly assigned per problem
- Results: N=2→87.4%, N=3→89.4%, N=5→89.9%, N=10→91.5%
- Surprise: all N≥2 ABOVE random (83.6%), not between coherent and random
- Phase transition N=1→2 is the key finding: one rule = compressible, two = not
- Files: `data/generate_math_multirule.py`, `run_multirule.sh`

## Theoretical Framework
Compression pressure = computational analogue of Popper's falsifiability.
- Type 1: Falsifiable true → predictions match → max compression
- Type 2: Falsifiable false → discrepancies memorized → poor compression
- Type 3a: Unfalsifiable (vague) → can't predict → intermediate
- Type 3b: Unfalsifiable (ad hoc escape hatches) → system grows → anti-compression

## NeurIPS Revision (2026-03-14)
- **П.1 (compression measure): DONE** — gzip/bz2 on completion-only segments, 9 conditions
  - Spearman ρ=0.68, p=0.042 between compression delta and paired accuracy
  - Files: `analysis/compression_measure.py`, `analysis/plot_compression_vs_accuracy.py`
  - Results: `results/compression_ratios.json`, `results/figure_compression_vs_accuracy.png`
  - Added to paper: Section 3.5 (Table C1, Figure C2)
- **П.2 (learning curves): DONE** — paired accuracy at intermediate checkpoints
  - All sizes reach behavioral plateau by step 3000-4000
  - Large model NOT undertrained: 88.8% at step 3000, stable through 5000
  - Files: `analysis/plot_learning_curves.py`, `results/learning_curves.json`, `results/figure_learning_curves.png`
  - Added to paper: Section 7.2 (Table LC1, Figure LC)
- **П.5 (convergence-matched): NOT NEEDED** — learning curves show plateau
- **П.6 (generation sanity check): DONE** — greedy decode + SymPy, 500 tasks × 8 models
  - Random-trained: 30.5%±1.7%, Coherent-trained: 20.8%±3.6%, paired t-test p=0.013
  - Arithmetic = 0% for both (too hard for tiny model generation)
  - Files: `training/eval_generation.py`, `run_generation_check.sh`, `run_generation_check_full.sh`
  - Added to paper: Section 8.4 (Table G1)
- **П.7 (NLL histogram): DONE** — per-pair NLL(inc)-NLL(cor) distribution
  - Random: right-skewed, mean=0.048 >> median=0.025, heavy tail
  - Coherent: symmetric around 0
  - Explains metric divergence (mean DLoss vs accuracy)
  - Files: `analysis/plot_nll_histogram.py`, `results/nll_pair_deltas.json`, `results/figure_nll_histogram.png`
  - Added to paper: Section 8.1 (Figure H1)
- **П.3 (BPE tokenization): DONE** — sentencepiece BPE vocab=1000, 8 models on MLX (no GPU needed!)
  - Random BPE: 85.6%±0.2% (vs char 83.1%±2.0%) — effect SURVIVES and STRENGTHENS
  - Coherent BPE: 45.9%±1.4% (vs char 47.2%±2.7%) — still no truth bias
  - Files: `training/tokenizer.py` (BPETokenizer class), `run_bpe_experiment.sh`, `results/bpe_*`
  - Added to paper: Section 8.4 (Table BPE1)
- **П.4 (text restructuring): DONE** — abstract 180 words, Discussion 3 themes, Conclusion 1 paragraph
- **ALL THREE VULNERABILITIES CLOSED. All mandatory experiments complete.**
- **Remaining:** П.8-9 (polish), П.11 (LaTeX)

## Current Phase: REWRITE around denoising framing (2026-03-15)
- **Decision:** rebuild paper with denoising as primary setup, arXiv will be updated
- NeurIPS NOT yet submitted, no constraints on restructuring
- Denoising framing: "model sees contradictory answers to same problem — what does it prefer?"
- Need to rerun: scaling (medium/large), multi-rule, BPE, synthetic world — all in denoising setup
- Original experiments → Supporting Evidence / Appendix
- Previously: PUBLISHED on arXiv (https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.11749)
- P0: Results integrity — **DONE**
  - `scripts/collect_results.py` → `results_master.csv` + `scripts/tables.md`
  - ΔLoss convention fixed (Tabl 3 sign error, Cond A was −0.0003→+0.0005)
  - Binomial test (16/16 seeds, p=3.05e-5) + bootstrap CI in all tables
  - `docs/refs_audit.md` — fixed DeMoss (fabricated), Pan (wrong authors), Ravfogel (wrong co-authors)
  - Contradictory eval run (4 seeds had no eval files)
- P1: Reframe thesis — **DONE**
  - New title: "Когда сжатие предпочитает истину?"
  - Abstract/intro rewritten with 3 caveats (text not world, frequency, description length)
  - Popper → Discussion only (section 7.2, interpretive analogy with limits)
  - Neutral condition names in experiments (no гомеопатия/астрология)
  - Contribution statement in Related Work
  - Missing refs added (Shannon, Solomonoff, Hutter)
- P2: Paired evaluation — **DONE** (28 models evaluated, 2026-03-06)
  - All paired evals complete: random (4 proportions), coherent, contradictory, C/D/E
  - C/D/E corpus-level ΔLoss was artifact — paired eval shows no truth bias
  - [ ] Length control, full-dataset eval, factor analysis
- P3: New experiments — scaling DONE, multi-rule DONE, synthetic world DONE
  - Figures 6+7 created: `analysis/plot_scaling_multirule.py` → `results/figure6_scaling.png`, `results/figure7_multirule.png`
  - Paper updated with large scaling + multi-rule sections
- P4: Novelty positioning, English translation, submission
- Key decision: title changed to "Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth" / "Сжатие предпочитает согласованность, а не истину"
- Named principle: **Compression–Consistency Principle** — gradient descent favors the most compressible hypothesis, not truth. Truth bias is a side effect of error incoherence.
- Conceptual figure added to Section 3.5: `results/figure_conceptual.png`, script `analysis/plot_conceptual.py`
- Intro rewritten: problem → existing explanations → our hypothesis → what we show

## User Preferences
- User wants historical examples alongside modern pseudoscience (phlogiston, miasma, geocentrism)
- Paper in Russian
- Don't add intermediate proportions (45/55, 65/35, etc.) — too much

1	"""
2	Paired evaluation in PyTorch.
3	
4	Port of training/eval_paired.py (MLX).
5	"""
6	
7	import argparse
8	import json
9	import math
10	import sys
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	import numpy as np
14	import torch
15	import torch.nn.functional as F
16	
17	from model import create_model
18	
19	sys.path.append(str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "training"))
20	from tokenizer import load_tokenizer
21	
22	
23	def completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids: list[int], completion_ids: list[int],
24	                         max_seq_len: int, device: str = "cuda") -> dict:
25	    full_ids = prompt_ids + completion_ids
26	    n_prompt = len(prompt_ids)
27	    n_completion = len(completion_ids)
28	
29	    if n_completion == 0:
30	        return {
31	            "mean_nll": float("nan"),
32	            "sum_nll": float("nan"),
33	            "token_losses": np.array([], dtype=float),
34	            "n_scored_tokens": 0,
35	            "n_completion_tokens": 0,
36	        }
37	
38	    if len(full_ids) > max_seq_len + 1:
39	        excess = len(full_ids) - (max_seq_len + 1)
40	        full_ids = full_ids[excess:]
41	        n_prompt = max(0, n_prompt - excess)
42	
43	    x = torch.tensor(full_ids[:-1], dtype=torch.long, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
44	    y = torch.tensor(full_ids[1:], dtype=torch.long, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
45	
46	    with torch.no_grad():
47	        logits = model(x)
48	
49	    T = logits.shape[1]
50	    V = logits.shape[2]
51	    ce = F.cross_entropy(logits.view(T, V), y.view(T), reduction="none")
52	
53	    start = max(0, n_prompt - 1)
54	    end = start + n_completion
55	    if end > T:
56	        end = T
57	    if start >= end:
58	        return {
59	            "mean_nll": float("nan"),
60	            "sum_nll": float("nan"),
61	            "token_losses": np.array([], dtype=float),
62	            "n_scored_tokens": 0,
63	            "n_completion_tokens": n_completion,
64	        }
65	
66	    completion_ce = ce[start:end]
67	    token_losses = completion_ce.cpu().numpy().astype(float)
68	    return {
69	        "mean_nll": float(token_losses.mean()),
70	        "sum_nll": float(token_losses.sum()),
71	        "token_losses": token_losses,
72	        "n_scored_tokens": int(token_losses.size),
73	        "n_completion_tokens": n_completion,
74	    }
75	
76	
77	def matched_prefix_mean(stats_a, stats_b):
78	    losses_a = stats_a["token_losses"]
79	    losses_b = stats_b["token_losses"]
80	    n = min(len(losses_a), len(losses_b))
81	    if n == 0:
82	        return float("nan"), float("nan")
83	    return float(losses_a[:n].mean()), float(losses_b[:n].mean())
84	
85	
86	def main():
87	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Paired evaluation (PyTorch)")
88	    parser.add_argument("--model-size", type=str, required=True)
89	    parser.add_argument("--weights", type=str, required=True)
90	    parser.add_argument("--tokenizer", type=str, required=True)
91	    parser.add_argument("--test-paired", type=str, required=True)
92	    parser.add_argument("--seq-len", type=int, default=256)
93	    parser.add_argument("--output", type=str, default=None)
94	    parser.add_argument("--device", type=str, default="cuda")
95	    args = parser.parse_args()
96	
97	    device = args.device
98	    tokenizer = load_tokenizer(args.tokenizer)
99	    model = create_model(args.model_size, tokenizer.vocab_size,
100	                         max_seq_len=args.seq_len, device=device)
101	    state = torch.load(args.weights, map_location=device, weights_only=True)
102	    model.load_state_dict(state)
103	    model.eval()
104	
105	    pairs = []
106	    with open(args.test_paired) as f:
107	        for line in f:
108	            pairs.append(json.loads(line))
109	
110	    print(f"Model: {args.model_size} | Weights: {args.weights}")
111	    print(f"Paired test problems: {len(pairs)}")
112	    print("-" * 60)
113	
114	    correct_nlls = []
115	    incorrect_nlls = []
116	    correct_wins = 0
117	    by_type = {}
118	
119	    for pair in pairs:
120	        prompt_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["prompt"])
121	        correct_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["correct_completion"])
122	        incorrect_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["incorrect_completion"])
123	
124	        c_stats = completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids, correct_ids, args.seq_len, device)
125	        i_stats = completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids, incorrect_ids, args.seq_len, device)
126	        c_nll = c_stats["mean_nll"]
127	        i_nll = i_stats["mean_nll"]
128	
129	        if math.isnan(c_nll) or math.isnan(i_nll):
130	            continue
131	
132	        correct_nlls.append(c_nll)
133	        incorrect_nlls.append(i_nll)
134	        if c_nll < i_nll:
135	            correct_wins += 1
136	
137	        ptype = pair.get("problem_type", "unknown")
138	        if ptype not in by_type:
139	            by_type[ptype] = {"correct_nlls": [], "incorrect_nlls": [], "correct_wins": 0}
140	        by_type[ptype]["correct_nlls"].append(c_nll)
141	        by_type[ptype]["incorrect_nlls"].append(i_nll)
142	        if c_nll < i_nll:
143	            by_type[ptype]["correct_wins"] += 1
144	
145	    n = len(correct_nlls)
146	    if n == 0:
147	        print("No valid pairs!")
148	        return
149	
150	    avg_correct = sum(correct_nlls) / n
151	    avg_incorrect = sum(incorrect_nlls) / n
152	    delta = avg_incorrect - avg_correct
153	    accuracy = correct_wins / n
154	
155	    print(f"\nAggregate ({n} pairs):")
156	    print(f"  Avg NLL correct:   {avg_correct:.4f}")
157	    print(f"  Avg NLL incorrect: {avg_incorrect:.4f}")
158	    print(f"  Delta (inc - cor): {delta:+.4f}")
159	    print(f"  Pair accuracy:     {accuracy:.3f} ({correct_wins}/{n})")
160	
161	    print(f"\nPer-type breakdown:")
162	    type_results = {}
163	    for ptype, data in sorted(by_type.items()):
164	        nt = len(data["correct_nlls"])
165	        avg_c = sum(data["correct_nlls"]) / nt
166	        avg_i = sum(data["incorrect_nlls"]) / nt
167	        d = avg_i - avg_c
168	        acc = data["correct_wins"] / nt
169	        print(f"  {ptype:12s}: delta={d:+.4f}  accuracy={acc:.3f} ({data['correct_wins']}/{nt})")
170	        type_results[ptype] = {
171	            "n": nt, "delta": d, "accuracy": acc, "correct_wins": data["correct_wins"],
172	        }
173	
174	    # Bootstrap CI
175	    rng = np.random.RandomState(42)
176	    correct_arr = np.array(correct_nlls)
177	    incorrect_arr = np.array(incorrect_nlls)
178	    pair_deltas = incorrect_arr - correct_arr
179	    deltas_boot = []
180	    for _ in range(10000):
181	        idx = rng.choice(n, size=n, replace=True)
182	        deltas_boot.append(pair_deltas[idx].mean())
183	    ci_lo, ci_hi = np.percentile(deltas_boot, [2.5, 97.5])
184	    print(f"\n  Bootstrap 95% CI for delta: [{ci_lo:+.4f}, {ci_hi:+.4f}]")
185

1	"""
2	Paired evaluation: same prompt, two completions (correct vs incorrect).
3	
4	For each paired problem:
5	1. Encode prompt + correct_completion and prompt + incorrect_completion
6	2. Compute NLL only on the completion tokens (conditioned on prompt)
7	3. Report per-pair and aggregate statistics
8	
9	Primary metric:
10	- mean NLL on completion tokens
11	
12	Auxiliary robustness metrics:
13	- sum NLL on completion tokens
14	- mean NLL on the first min(len(correct), len(incorrect)) scored completion tokens
15	
16	This removes the confound of different prompts between correct/incorrect test sets.
17	"""
18	
19	import argparse
20	import json
21	import math
22	from pathlib import Path
23	
24	import mlx.core as mx
25	import mlx.nn as nn
26	import numpy as np
27	
28	from model import create_model
29	from tokenizer import CharTokenizer, load_tokenizer
30	
31	
32	def completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids: list[int], completion_ids: list[int],
33	                         max_seq_len: int) -> dict[str, object]:
34	    """Compute completion-token NLL statistics, conditioned on prompt.
35	
36	    Runs the full sequence through the model, but only computes loss
37	    on positions corresponding to completion tokens.
38	    """
39	    full_ids = prompt_ids + completion_ids
40	    n_prompt = len(prompt_ids)
41	    n_completion = len(completion_ids)
42	
43	    if n_completion == 0:
44	        return {
45	            "mean_nll": float("nan"),
46	            "sum_nll": float("nan"),
47	            "token_losses": np.array([], dtype=float),
48	            "n_scored_tokens": 0,
49	            "n_completion_tokens": 0,
50	        }
51	
52	    # Truncate if needed
53	    if len(full_ids) > max_seq_len + 1:
54	        # Keep as much prompt context as possible, truncate from the start
55	        excess = len(full_ids) - (max_seq_len + 1)
56	        full_ids = full_ids[excess:]
57	        n_prompt = max(0, n_prompt - excess)
58	
59	    # Input: all tokens except last; Target: all tokens except first
60	    x = mx.array(full_ids[:-1], dtype=mx.int32)[None, :]  # (1, T)
61	    y = mx.array(full_ids[1:], dtype=mx.int32)[None, :]    # (1, T)
62	
63	    logits = model(x)  # (1, T, V)
64	
65	    # Cross-entropy per position
66	    T = logits.shape[1]
67	    V = logits.shape[2]
68	    ce = nn.losses.cross_entropy(
69	        logits.reshape(T, V), y.reshape(T), reduction="none"
70	    )  # (T,)
71	
72	    # Only take loss on completion positions
73	    # Position i in ce corresponds to predicting token i+1 from tokens 0..i
74	    # Completion tokens start at index n_prompt in full_ids
75	    # So we want ce[n_prompt-1 : n_prompt-1+n_completion] (predicting completion given prompt)
76	    start = max(0, n_prompt - 1)
77	    end = start + n_completion
78	    if end > T:
79	        end = T
80	    if start >= end:
81	        return {
82	            "mean_nll": float("nan"),
83	            "sum_nll": float("nan"),
84	            "token_losses": np.array([], dtype=float),
85	            "n_scored_tokens": 0,
86	            "n_completion_tokens": n_completion,
87	        }
88	
89	    completion_ce = ce[start:end]
90	    token_losses = np.array(completion_ce.tolist(), dtype=float)
91	    return {
92	        "mean_nll": float(token_losses.mean()),
93	        "sum_nll": float(token_losses.sum()),
94	        "token_losses": token_losses,
95	        "n_scored_tokens": int(token_losses.size),
96	        "n_completion_tokens": n_completion,
97	    }
98	
99	
100	def matched_prefix_mean(stats_a: dict[str, object], stats_b: dict[str, object]) -> tuple[float, float]:
101	    """Compare mean NLL on the shared minimum number of scored completion tokens."""
102	    losses_a = stats_a["token_losses"]
103	    losses_b = stats_b["token_losses"]
104	    n = min(len(losses_a), len(losses_b))
105	    if n == 0:
106	        return float("nan"), float("nan")
107	    return float(losses_a[:n].mean()), float(losses_b[:n].mean())
108	
109	
110	def main():
111	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Paired evaluation")
112	    parser.add_argument("--model-size", type=str, required=True)
113	    parser.add_argument("--weights", type=str, required=True)
114	    parser.add_argument("--tokenizer", type=str, required=True)
115	    parser.add_argument("--test-paired", type=str, required=True,
116	                        help="JSONL file with paired test data")
117	    parser.add_argument("--seq-len", type=int, default=256)
118	    parser.add_argument("--output", type=str, default=None)
119	    args = parser.parse_args()
120	
121	    # Load model
122	    tokenizer = load_tokenizer(args.tokenizer)
123	    model = create_model(args.model_size, tokenizer.vocab_size, max_seq_len=args.seq_len)
124	    model.load_weights(args.weights)
125	    mx.eval(model.parameters())
126	
127	    # Load paired test data
128	    pairs = []
129	    with open(args.test_paired) as f:
130	        for line in f:
131	            pairs.append(json.loads(line))
132	
133	    print(f"Model: {args.model_size} | Weights: {args.weights}")
134	    print(f"Paired test problems: {len(pairs)}")
135	    print("-" * 60)
136	
137	    correct_nlls = []
138	    incorrect_nlls = []
139	    correct_sum_nlls = []
140	    incorrect_sum_nlls = []
141	    correct_matched_mean_nlls = []
142	    incorrect_matched_mean_nlls = []
143	    completion_lengths_correct = []
144	    completion_lengths_incorrect = []
145	    scored_lengths_correct = []
146	    scored_lengths_incorrect = []
147	    correct_wins = 0
148	    correct_sum_wins = 0
149	    correct_matched_mean_wins = 0
150	    by_type = {}
151	
152	    for pair in pairs:
153	        prompt_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["prompt"])
154	        correct_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["correct_completion"])
155	        incorrect_ids = tokenizer.encode(pair["incorrect_completion"])
156	
157	        c_stats = completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids, correct_ids, args.seq_len)
158	        i_stats = completion_nll_stats(model, prompt_ids, incorrect_ids, args.seq_len)
159	        c_nll = c_stats["mean_nll"]
160	        i_nll = i_stats["mean_nll"]
161	        c_sum = c_stats["sum_nll"]
162	        i_sum = i_stats["sum_nll"]
163	        c_matched, i_matched = matched_prefix_mean(c_stats, i_stats)
164	
165	        if any(math.isnan(x) for x in [c_nll, i_nll, c_sum, i_sum, c_matched, i_matched]):
166	            continue
167	
168	        correct_nlls.append(c_nll)
169	        incorrect_nlls.append(i_nll)
170	        correct_sum_nlls.append(c_sum)
171	        incorrect_sum_nlls.append(i_sum)
172	        correct_matched_mean_nlls.append(c_matched)
173	        incorrect_matched_mean_nlls.append(i_matched)
174	        completion_lengths_correct.append(c_stats["n_completion_tokens"])
175	        completion_lengths_incorrect.append(i_stats["n_completion_tokens"])
176	        scored_lengths_correct.append(c_stats["n_scored_tokens"])
177	        scored_lengths_incorrect.append(i_stats["n_scored_tokens"])
178	
179	        if c_nll < i_nll:
180	            correct_wins += 1
181	        if c_sum < i_sum:
182	            correct_sum_wins += 1
183	        if c_matched < i_matched:
184	            correct_matched_mean_wins += 1
185	
186	        ptype = pair.get("problem_type", "unknown")
187	        if ptype not in by_type:
188	            by_type[ptype] = {
189	                "correct_nlls": [],
190	                "incorrect_nlls": [],
191	                "correct_sum_nlls": [],
192	                "incorrect_sum_nlls": [],
193	                "correct_matched_mean_nlls": [],
194	                "incorrect_matched_mean_nlls": [],
195	                "correct_wins": 0,
196	                "correct_sum_wins": 0,
197	                "correct_matched_mean_wins": 0,
198	            }
199	        by_type[ptype]["correct_nlls"].append(c_nll)
200	        by_type[ptype]["incorrect_nlls"].append(i_nll)
201	        by_type[ptype]["correct_sum_nlls"].append(c_sum)
202	        by_type[ptype]["incorrect_sum_nlls"].append(i_sum)
203	        by_type[ptype]["correct_matched_mean_nlls"].append(c_matched)
204	        by_type[ptype]["incorrect_matched_mean_nlls"].append(i_matched)
205	        if c_nll < i_nll:
206	            by_type[ptype]["correct_wins"] += 1
207	        if c_sum < i_sum:
208	            by_type[ptype]["correct_sum_wins"] += 1
209	        if c_matched < i_matched:
210	            by_type[ptype]["correct_matched_mean_wins"] += 1
211	
212	    n = len(correct_nlls)
213	    if n == 0:
214	        print("No valid pairs!")
215	        return
216	
217	    avg_correct = sum(correct_nlls) / n
218	    avg_incorrect = sum(incorrect_nlls) / n
219	    delta = avg_incorrect - avg_correct
220	    accuracy = correct_wins / n
221	    avg_correct_sum = sum(correct_sum_nlls) / n
222	    avg_incorrect_sum = sum(incorrect_sum_nlls) / n
223	    delta_sum = avg_incorrect_sum - avg_correct_sum
224	    sum_accuracy = correct_sum_wins / n
225	    avg_correct_matched = sum(correct_matched_mean_nlls) / n
226	    avg_incorrect_matched = sum(incorrect_matched_mean_nlls) / n
227	    delta_matched = avg_incorrect_matched - avg_correct_matched
228	    matched_accuracy = correct_matched_mean_wins / n
229	
230	    print(f"\nAggregate ({n} pairs):")
231	    print(f"  Avg NLL correct:   {avg_correct:.4f}")
232	    print(f"  Avg NLL incorrect: {avg_incorrect:.4f}")
233	    print(f"  Delta (inc - cor): {delta:+.4f}")
234	    print(f"  Pair accuracy:     {accuracy:.3f} ({correct_wins}/{n})")
235	    print(f"  Sum-NLL delta:     {delta_sum:+.4f}  accuracy={sum_accuracy:.3f} ({correct_sum_wins}/{n})")
236	    print(
237	        f"  Length-matched mean delta: {delta_matched:+.4f}  "
238	        f"accuracy={matched_accuracy:.3f} ({correct_matched_mean_wins}/{n})"
239	    )
240	
241	    # Per-type breakdown
242	    print(f"\nPer-type breakdown:")
243	    type_results = {}
244	    for ptype, data in sorted(by_type.items()):
245	        nt = len(data["correct_nlls"])
246	        avg_c = sum(data["correct_nlls"]) / nt
247	        avg_i = sum(data["incorrect_nlls"]) / nt
248	        d = avg_i - avg_c
249	        acc = data["correct_wins"] / nt
250	        avg_c_sum = sum(data["correct_sum_nlls"]) / nt
251	        avg_i_sum = sum(data["incorrect_sum_nlls"]) / nt
252	        avg_c_matched = sum(data["correct_matched_mean_nlls"]) / nt
253	        avg_i_matched = sum(data["incorrect_matched_mean_nlls"]) / nt
254	        print(
255	            f"  {ptype:12s}: delta={d:+.4f}  accuracy={acc:.3f} "
256	            f"({data['correct_wins']}/{nt})"
257	        )
258	        type_results[ptype] = {
259	            "n": nt,
260	            "avg_correct_nll": avg_c,
261	            "avg_incorrect_nll": avg_i,
262	            "delta": d,
263	            "accuracy": acc,
264	            "correct_wins": data["correct_wins"],
265	            "avg_correct_sum_nll": avg_c_sum,
266	            "avg_incorrect_sum_nll": avg_i_sum,
267	            "sum_delta": avg_i_sum - avg_c_sum,
268	            "sum_accuracy": data["correct_sum_wins"] / nt,
269	            "sum_correct_wins": data["correct_sum_wins"],
270	            "avg_correct_matched_mean_nll": avg_c_matched,
271	            "avg_incorrect_matched_mean_nll": avg_i_matched,
272	            "matched_mean_delta": avg_i_matched - avg_c_matched,
273	            "matched_mean_accuracy": data["correct_matched_mean_wins"] / nt,
274	            "matched_mean_correct_wins": data["correct_matched_mean_wins"],
275	        }
276	
277	    # Bootstrap CI for delta
278	    rng = np.random.RandomState(42)
279	    deltas_boot = []
280	    correct_arr = np.array(correct_nlls)
281	    incorrect_arr = np.array(incorrect_nlls)
282	    pair_deltas = incorrect_arr - correct_arr
283	    for _ in range(10000):
284	        idx = rng.choice(n, size=n, replace=True)
285	        deltas_boot.append(pair_deltas[idx].mean())
286	    ci_lo, ci_hi = np.percentile(deltas_boot, [2.5, 97.5])
287	
288	    print(f"\n  Bootstrap 95% CI for delta: [{ci_lo:+.4f}, {ci_hi:+.4f}]")
289	
290	    # Wilcoxon signed-rank test
291	    from scipy import stats as scipy_stats
292	    stat, p_value = scipy_stats.wilcoxon(pair_deltas, alternative='greater')
293	    print(f"  Wilcoxon signed-rank test: W={stat:.0f}, p={p_value:.6f}")
294	
295	    results = {
296	        "model_size": args.model_size,
297	        "weights": args.weights,
298	        "n_pairs": n,
299	        "avg_correct_nll": avg_correct,
300	        "avg_incorrect_nll": avg_incorrect,
301	        "delta": delta,
302	        "pair_accuracy": accuracy,
303	        "correct_wins": correct_wins,
304	        "bootstrap_ci_95": [ci_lo, ci_hi],
305	        "wilcoxon_p": p_value,
306	        "robustness": {
307	            "sum_nll": {
308	                "avg_correct": avg_correct_sum,
309	                "avg_incorrect": avg_incorrect_sum,
310	                "delta": delta_sum,
311	                "pair_accuracy": sum_accuracy,
312	                "correct_wins": correct_sum_wins,
313	            },
314	            "length_matched_mean_nll": {
315	                "avg_correct": avg_correct_matched,
316	                "avg_incorrect": avg_incorrect_matched,

319	                "correct_wins": correct_matched_mean_wins,
320	            },

324	                "correct_min": int(min(completion_lengths_correct)),

326	                "correct_max": int(max(completion_lengths_correct)),
327	                "incorrect_max": int(max(completion_lengths_incorrect)),
328	                "correct_scored_mean": float(np.mean(scored_lengths_correct)),
329	                "incorrect_scored_mean": float(np.mean(scored_lengths_incorrect)),
330	            },
331	        },
332	        "by_type": type_results,
333	    }
334	
335	    if args.output:
336	        Path(args.output).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
337	        with open(args.output, "w") as f:
338	            json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
339	        print(f"\nSaved to {args.output}")
340
